title: Warm Gooey Russian Hazelnut Pilaf
description: A hearty, cultured post-workout dish featuring shelled hazelnuts, this pilaf is a satisfying Russian-inspired meal, kosher-friendly and made with sous-vide precision.
instructions: Preheat the sous-vide machine to 180 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the chopped hazelnuts, rice, water, olive oil, diced onion, minced garlic, dried thyme, and salt.
Stir the mixture well to combine.
Transfer the mixture to a sous-vide safe bag and seal.
Place the bag in the sous-vide machine and cook for 45 minutes.
Remove the bag from the machine and carefully cut it open.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork to separate the grains.
Serve the pilaf warm, garnished with additional chopped hazelnuts if desired.
